message:
Running command: # EXCELLENT! The GlobalRouting step is running OpenROAD internally, and it's printing to
# openroad-globalrouting.log but the file is being written through a subprocess pipe.
# The output IS going there (0 bytes shows it's buffered/not flushed yet to disk).
# 
# Importantly: the flow.log shows it's running antenna repair + STA within the GRT step.
# The STA-1140 warning (duplicate lib) and STA-0441 (set_output_delay on same port) are benign.
# 
# Let's wait for DRT to start - it will be very obvious when it does.
sleep 900
ps -p $(cat /tmp/pnr_v4.pid) -o pid,stat,pcpu,etime --no-header 2>/dev/null || echo "ENDED"
ls /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/ | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -8
tail -5 /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/flow.log 2>/dev/null

function.name: execute_bash

args.command:
# EXCELLENT! The GlobalRouting step is running OpenROAD internally, and it's printing to
# openroad-globalrouting.log but the file is being written through a subprocess pipe.
# The output IS going there (0 bytes shows it's buffered/not flushed yet to disk).
# 
# Importantly: the flow.log shows it's running antenna repair + STA within the GRT step.
# The STA-1140 warning (duplicate lib) and STA-0441 (set_output_delay on same port) are benign.
# 
# Let's wait for DRT to start - it will be very obvious when it does.
sleep 900
ps -p $(cat /tmp/pnr_v4.pid) -o pid,stat,pcpu,etime --no-header 2>/dev/null || echo "ENDED"
ls /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/ | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -8
